phase3_findings.description: flip-review-progress-failed.sh's id-lookup arm converges three distinct conditions (rc 2 absent, rc 1 gh-api/parse failure, empty CID) onto one 'comment-absent no-op' breadcrumb, misdiagnosing a failed read as a missing comment.

devflow.yml's flip step fires only on steps.claude.outcome failure/cancelled; it misses the engine-is_error-on-success arm that finalize_check covers, which the issue's Desired Behavior explicitly names. Originally graded LOW by the agent; calibrated up.

The devflow.yml pin greps 'steps.claude.outcome', which also matches the step's own CLAUDE_OUTCOME: env line, so deleting a disjunct from the flip step's if: guard leaves the pin GREEN — a vacuous coverage pin on an AC-named behavior (cancelled).
flip-review-progress-failed.sh's missing-args and body-read-failure arms have no test; the stub has no knob to fail the body read.
workpad.py's --status argparse help enumerates only three glyphs (🚀/🎉/👎), stale after 💥 joined the vocabulary.
stall-backstop-decide.sh's CLASS doc enumerates terminal as '🎉 Complete / 👎 Blocked (healthy end)', omitting 💥 Failed and mislabelling a dead end as healthy.
run.sh's 'its four fail-loud call sites' is an exact-count mirror-fact comment; repo convention prefers a lower bound.
The helper header's arm enumeration reads as exhaustive but omits the missing-args and no-Status arms.

fix_decisions.claim_text: devflow.yml flip step misses the engine is_error arm
fix_decisions.evidence: Agent graded it LOW/informational. Calibrated UP, not down: engine_is_error is parsed AFTER the claude step (devflow-runner.yml:600-632), so a step can exit success with is_error=true — the arm is genuinely reachable, and the issue's Desired Behavior names 'engine is_error' among the three non-success arms. This is a fail-open coverage hole in a guard this diff itself introduced, so the park-calibration gate's shape 1 forbids parking it.

fix_decisions.claim_text: The helper's Status-line regex and its interim test startswith('🚀') assume the seeded progress comment carries a line exactly of the form '**Status:** 🚀 Reviewing'. A different Status-line format in the seed defeats the flip.
fix_decisions.evidence: The verifier's own evidence refutes its FAIL verdict: it demonstrated the regex ^\*\*Status:\*\*[ \t]*(.*)$ (re.MULTILINE) plus m.group(1).lstrip().startswith('🚀') tolerates absent/multiple spaces after the colon and arbitrary text after the glyph. The code is correct; only the checklist claim's word 'exactly' is wrong. Per the verifier contract ('when a claim's wording is technically inaccurate but the underlying code is correct, prefer PASS'), this should have been a PASS. Additionally confirmed the transform rewrites only the FIRST **Status:** match (re.search + slice), which is the front-matter line, so a later quoted **Status:** in a report body cannot be clobbered.
